concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: Mexico City leads Mexico's urban system with 18.6% of tracked urban population across 1013 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Guadalajara & Zapopan
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Guadalajara and Zapopan (7 km apart, combined population 7,001,491). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 14 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Mexico sits between distributed and dominant: Mexico City leads with 18.6% but doesn't overwhelm. Gravity clusters tightly around Guadalajara → Zapopan (14 km avg) — a strong sub-regional core coexists with the national leader, creating a layered urban hierarchy.
